MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Answer
Off Calendar Jul-15-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT RAPHAEL STONE CA, INC. FKA CALIFORNIA QUARTZ, INC.'s MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Answer.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Raphael Stone CA Inc.'s (Raphael) motion for leave to file an amended answer is granted.

Raphael has answered on behalf of California Quartz Inc. under the assumption that California Quartz was its former name but has recently learned California Quartz was a separate entity. Raphael also seeks to correct some citations noted in its previous answer. Plaintiff opposes, contending that allowing the amendment to occur will cause California Quartz to disappear from the case.

Courts liberally allow amendments to answers. (Hulsey v. Koehler (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1150, 1159 ["In particular, liberality should be displayed in allowing amendments to answers, for a defendant denied leave to amend is permanently deprived of a defense."].) If California Quartz is a separate entity, as Raphael's exhibits show and Plaintiff does not contest, then it is unjust and untenable to bind it to an answer it did not make. There is no apparent obstacle to Plaintiff serving California Quartz and bringing it into the case.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT CAESARSTONE USA, INC.'s Motion For Order Allowing Lydecker To Withdraw Its Notice Of Appearance Made On Behalf Of Defendant Caesarstone, Ltd.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The "motion for order allowing Lydecker to withdraw its notice of appearance made on behalf of defendant Caesarstone, Ltd." is denied. The motion relies solely on CCP 473(b), but a notice of appearance is not a "judgment, dismissal, order" or "proceeding." (Id.) Moreover, no "mistake" is shown. (Id.; Mot. 4:22-25.)

Order signed.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT CAESARSTONE LTD's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Caesarstone, Ltd.'s "motion to quash service of summons" is denied for two reasons. First, Caesarstone's deadline for filing such a motion was December 11, 2023, but Caesarstone did not file until February 5, 2024. (CCP 418.10; Obj. 2:7-23.) Second, such a motion must be filed before a party generally appears in an action and Caesarstone generally appeared on January 16, 2024. (CCP 1014; Roy v. Sup. Ct. (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 337, 341; Obj. 2:25-4:8.)

Order signed.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-05-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. DAN MORSE, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP (Tel: 415-898-1555) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. LITAL RUIMY, Esq. of WFBM, LLP (Tel: 213-489-4820) for Defendants Francini Inc.; Parsoda U.S.A., Inc; and Evolv Surfaces, Inc.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT EVOLV SURFACES, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter discussed. The Court notes Counsel Ruimy failed to contest the tentative ruling in a timely fashion. Counsel Ruimy explains why she failed to contest in a timely fashion. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Evolv Surfaces, Inc.'s motion to strike is denied. Allegations, as here, that a defendant intentionally concealed hazards of its product suffice to allow punitive damages. (Vosler v. Richards Manufacturing Co. (1983) 143 Cal.App.3d 952, 966; Civ, Code 3294.) Defendant argues for specificity in pleading of a claim - fraudulent concealment - that by its nature does not lend itself to that, as defendant has allegedly concealed the facts. (Alfaro v. Community Housing Improvement System & Planning Assn., Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384.) Further, the at-issue products are adequately pled.

Counsel for the prevailing is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Mar-05-2024 Overruled

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. DAN MORSE, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP (Tel: 415-898-1555) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. LITAL RUIMY, Esq. of WFBM, LLP (Tel: 213-489-4820) for Defendants Francini Inc.; Parsoda U.S.A., Inc; and Evolv Surfaces, Inc.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT FRANCINI INC.'s DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Matter discussed. The Court notes Counsel Ruimy failed to contest the tentative ruling in a timely fashion. Counsel Ruimy explains why she failed to contest in a timely fashion. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Francini Inc.'s demurrer to the complaint's fourth cause of action (fraudulent concealment) is overruled. As detailed in plaintiffs' opposition, the complaint adequately pleads duties to disclose under multiple theories. (Warner Constr. Corp v. City of Los Angeles (1970) 2 Cal.3d 285. 294; Jones v. ConocoPhillips Co. (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 1187.) Defendant argues that the corporation's role and intent must be pled "with specificity," but the fraud, being concealed, is far better known to defendant than to plaintiffs. (Alfaro v. Community Housing Improvement System & Planning Assn., Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384.)

Counsel for the prevailing is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT EVOLV SURFACES, INC.'s DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Matter discussed. The Court notes Counsel Ruimy failed to contest the tentative ruling in a timely fashion. Counsel Ruimy explains why she failed to contest in a timely fashion. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Evolv Surfaces, Inc.'s demurrer to the complaint's fourth cause of action (fraudulent concealment) is overruled. Defendant argues that the corporation's role and intent must be pled "with specificity," but the fraud, being concealed, is far better known to defendant than to plaintiffs. (Alfaro v. Community Housing Improvement System & Planning Assn., Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384.)

Counsel for the prevailing is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jan-03-2024 Continued To Apr-03-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. GILBERT L. PURCELL, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP (Tel: 415-898-1555) for plaintiffs;
Atty. MICHAEL A. HALE, Esq. of Latham & Watkins LLP (Tel: 213-485-1234) for specially appearing defendant Stylenquaza LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT STYLENQUAZA LLC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The hearing on Stylenquaza, Inc.'s (defendant) "motion to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction" is continued to April 3, 2024.

Plaintiffs allege that they developed silicosis from fabricating countertops. Defendant (one of 41 putative defendants) is a Texas LLC that distributes slabs supplied by a Vietnamese entity. (Nguyen Dec. 2:12-16.)

Defendant manager/owner An Nguyen declares that its distribution is "primarily" outside California. (Id. at 2:22-26.) However, he acknowledges some California contacts. First, on "very rare occasions," defendant sells "quartz slabs" to another distributor that sells into California. (Id. at 2:6-10.) Second, on "rare occasions" between 2018 and 2022, defendant "distributed insubstantial amounts of pre-fabricated tops directly to California." (Id. at 2:11-12.) Third, defendant "also supplied, via six (6) shipments to California, 22 total stone slabs." (Id. at 2:14-16.)

Also, plaintiffs contend that defendant "has a website" listing regional distributors, but does not establish that the website is, indeed, defendant's or much else about it.

Given the ambiguous nature of both sides' presentations, plaintiffs' request for jurisdictional discovery is granted. (See Magnecomp Corp. v. Athene Co. (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 526, 533.)

As stated above, the hearing of this motion is continued to April 3. Supplemental briefs (maximum five pages) shall be filed by March 21.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
